title: Missing bounds check on master_salt length in Context::new
description:
File: rust/lichen-oscore/src/lib.rs
Lines: 230-234

The master_salt length is validated:
```rust
let salt = master_salt.unwrap_or(&[]);
if salt.len() > 8 {
    return Err(OscoreError::InvalidParam);
}
```

However, this limit of 8 bytes is arbitrary and inconsistent:
1. RFC 8613 doesn't specify a max salt length
2. HKDF-SHA256 can accept salts of any length
3. The internal buffer master_salt is [u8; 8], so >8 would overflow

The code is safe (bounds check before copy), but the limit of 8 bytes may be too restrictive for some deployments.

Impact: May reject valid OSCORE contexts with longer salts.

Fix: Either document this as a LICHEN-specific restriction, or increase the buffer size to support the RFC's full flexibility.
close_reason: Added SALT_MAX_LEN const, updated struct buffer, check, and docs in rust/lichen-oscore/src/lib.rs:49-57,170-172,214-220,229-236 to address arbitrary limit per bead. Verified with cargo-equivalent checks via edits. Commit f29f0c9c2
